RULING: PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR ORDER ADVANCING THE CURRENT TRIAL DATE WITH PREFERENCE; AND EXTENDING DISCOVERY CUTOFF
Not argued and tentative ruling amended to correct hearing dates: Plaintiff's Motion for Order Advancing the Current Trial Date with Preference; and Extending Discovery Cutoff is GRANTED pursuant to C.C.P. 36(a). No opposition filed. The current trial date of April 13, 2020 is vacated. The new trial date is February 18, 2020 at 11:15 a.m. in Department 503. Last day pursuant to C.C.P. 36(f) is February 26, 2020. Local Rule 20 shall be followed by the parties as well as all San Francisco Local Rules and California Rules of Court. The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is February 13, 2020. Summary judgment/adjudication motions shall be brought on regular notice pursuant to the relevant provisions of the C.C.P., unless the parties stipulate otherwise. The Court will allow a maximum of four summary judgment/adjudication motions per day to be calendared in this action. Before a party files and serves such motion, it must contact the Court's Calendar Clerk to make a reservation. Time to respond to written discovery that has not yet been served is shortened to 20 days. For written discovery that has already been served, responses are due within 20 days of this hearing or by the date determined by the C.C.P., whichever is earlier. If there is any kind of issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, such meet and confer shall not be made via email or letter. It shall occur in person or via telephone. Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service. Fact discovery cut-off date is January 31, 2020. Expert discovery cut-off date is February 14, 2020. All bankruptcy documents shall be turned over to Defendants no later than November 29, 2019. If Plaintiff submits documents to bankruptcy trusts after this date, he must notify Defendants of this no later than five days after submission. The fact discovery cut-off does not apply to bankruptcy documents.

Off Calendar Dec-20-2016 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Daniel Solomon, Esq. of DENTONS US LLP (415.267.4000) for defendant & moving party;
Tiffany Viveros, Esq. of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415.898.1555), for plaintiffs and opposing party.

RULING:
DEFENDANT CERTAINTEED CORPORATION MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

Neither party contested the court's tentative ruling which was adopted: Defendant Certainteed Corporation's motion for summary adjudication of plaintiff's claim for punitive damages is denied. Assuming defendant sustained its initial burden, plaintiff presented evidence to create a triable issue whether defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. Such evidence includes the testimony of Lloyd Ambler (Exhibits B, G, P, R and T) and Leon Horowitz (Exhibits H, I), Views and Arguments document (Exhibit C), defendant's responses to interrogatories (Exhibit A) as well as Exhibits K, L, N, Q and S. While plaintiff does not need to prove his case for punitive damages at the summary adjudication stage, the Court takes the higher proof standard into consideration. Still, the Court cannot conclude that no reasonable jury could find plaintiff's evidence to be clear and convincing proof of malice, fraud or oppression. (See American Airlines, Inc. v. Sheppard, Mullin, Richter & Hampton (2002) 96 Cal.App.4th 1017; Hoch v. Allied-Signal, Inc. (1994) 24 Cal.App.4th 48.) Order signed in open court. Judge: Garrett L. Wong; Matter not reported.
